limits: This report summarizes finite computational scans. It is not a proof of the Collatz conjecture.
meaning: Different hardness lenses identify different finite stress cases. This is not a contradiction. It means trajectory length, recovery distance, compensation surplus, and adversarial compensation hardness are distinct observables.

metrics.description: Longest post-chain recovery distance in the critical frontier scan.
metrics.interpretation: Measures recovery distance after structurally cheap debt chains.
Measures how hard a bad finite window is to compensate.

metrics.description: Largest hardness score under adversarial candidate generation.
metrics.interpretation: Measures the strongest adversarial finite compensation stress case.

metrics.description: Smallest positive surplus observed in the adversarial compensation scan.
metrics.interpretation: Measures the tightest still-positive compensation margin found.

metrics.description: Known long-trajectory anchor included as a comparison baseline.
metrics.interpretation: Long trajectory length is not the same hardness notion as compensation hardness.
